message:
chore(deps): quick-xml 0.37 -> 0.39 + track unescape rename

Bumps the `quick-xml` dependency used by fnx-readwrite's GraphML
parser from 0.37.5 to 0.39.2 and adapts the two call sites whose
method rename is the only API break in that window.

crates/fnx-readwrite/Cargo.toml:
- `quick-xml = "0.37.5"` -> `quick-xml = "0.39.2"`. No feature
  selectors or default-features flags were in play; the dep stays
  on the default feature set the GraphML EdgeListEngine uses.

crates/fnx-readwrite/src/lib.rs (EdgeListEngine text handler):
- `e.unescape()` -> `e.xml10_content()` at the two `Event::Text`
  branches (`current_data_key.is_some() && !current_data_has_children`
  and `current_key_default_key.is_some()`). quick-xml 0.38 renamed
  the XML 1.0 content-unescape helper from `unescape` to
  `xml10_content` to disambiguate it from attribute-mode unescape
  rules; the behavior on XML 1.0 character references is
  equivalent. Error handling, warning strings ("graphml data text
  unescape error" / "graphml default text unescape error"), and
  the `current_*_text.push_str(&text)` accumulation all remain
  untouched so GraphML parsing surface semantics are unchanged.

Cargo.lock:
- Drops `quick-xml 0.37.5` resolution, picks up `quick-xml 0.39.2`
  plus its compatible transitive dep resolution. No consumer-side
  major bumps fall out of this change.

Zero functional drift -- just the literal method rename and the
version pin. Any deeper quick-xml 0.38-> 0.39 changes (escape /
attribute serialization APIs) don't affect the GraphML read
pipeline because it only ever constructs `Event::Text` and reads
the content form we just migrated.
